engineering.translation: Even the senior engineer who has practiced attention-discipline for a decade still encounters days when her concentration collapses. Even the practitioner who is actively striving — who has architected her calendar around deep work, who closes Slack during focused intervals, who has memorised the runbook for her own interruption-recovery — can have her attention violently dispossessed by whichever surface pulls hardest in a given moment. The verse refuses two adjacent flatteries simultaneously. It refuses the apprentice's flattery that exertion alone suffices. It refuses the senior's flattery that fluency, once acquired, remains intact without renewal. The senses are pramāthīni — churning — meaning not merely powerful but actively agitating, doing antagonistic work against the operator's settled posture. The wise are not immune; the striving are not immune; the long-experienced are not immune. Disciplined attention is a posture sustained moment-by-moment, not an achievement banked. Vigilance is what the steady mind costs, and it is repaid daily by the same person who paid it yesterday, with no carryover.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer with twelve years on infrastructure teams has been running her attention-system for the last three of them. She catalogues her failure modes precisely: Slack at 09:14 dismantles her morning, Datadog open during code review fragments her review cadence, GitHub notifications routed to email rather than push prevent the immediate-response reflex. She maintains a runbook for her own concentration. On a particular Tuesday, halfway through preparing a Postgres schema migration, a Slack ping fires from a junior engineer asking about an unrelated Kubernetes deployment. The discipline-channel is muted; she does not respond. Forty minutes later, she discovers her browser tab now sits on the Kubernetes incident channel, she has read three threads in detail, and the migration-prep document is still scrolled where she left it. The transition was not consciously chosen. The pramāthīni — the churning quality of the senses — caught her by an unguarded structural edge: a senior-engineer reflex to assist, an operator-empathy for a colleague mid-incident, a curiosity about whether the unrelated deployment might in fact touch her own service through some upstream dependency. Her striving (yatataḥ) was genuine; her discrimination (vipaścitaḥ) was genuine; the senses nonetheless carried the mind off (haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ). This verse names that exact failure mode and refuses to call her incompetent for experiencing it. It says: this is structurally what happens. Plan around it. The structural plan arrives in 2.61.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader uses it to justify giving up on attention-discipline because 'even the wise lose'. The verse is the opposite move: it warns that vigilance is required precisely because effort alone is insufficient. If an engineer reads this verse and concludes she should stop closing Slack, stop blocking calendar time, stop muting GitHub — she has read the verse backwards. The verse tightens the requirement, it does not relax it.
engineering.counter_example: The verse does not apply to the engineer who has not yet done the apprentice work — the one who has never structured her attention surfaces, who has never closed Slack, who has never run a deep-work block. To that engineer, the warning 'even the striving fail' would be premature. She has not yet entered the regime where the verse applies. Krishna names the wise (vipaścitaḥ) and the striving (yatataḥ) specifically; the verse is scoped to the practitioner, not the beginner. The beginner needs the small water-pond first.
engineering.implication: Treat your attention-discipline as a daily practice, not a settled fact. The same person who held focus yesterday must hold it again today, against the same pull, with no carryover credit. Plan structurally for the moments when the senses will catch you, because they will.
engineering.quotable_line: Even the senior who has done this for ten years still has bad days. The verse refuses to call her bad at her job for it.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack (vedabase.io Prabhupada + holy-bhagavad-gita.org Mukundananda + gitasupersite.iitk.ac.in Devanagari + bhagavad-gita.us Shankara excerpt + GRETIL critical edition). Anchored on Shankara's reading that api binds either to yatataḥ or vipaścitaḥ (both 'striving' and 'wise' are vulnerable) and the active churning sense of pramāthīni. Engineering analog names the staff engineer who has done attention-discipline for years and still has the senses carry her mind away — ongoing vigilance, not achieved state. Named tools: Slack, Datadog, GitHub, Postgres, Kubernetes. Counter-example scopes the verse to practitioners, not beginners.

Single mutation: lifted FK grade and lexical diversity by replacing repeated short Anglo-Saxon words ('done', 'has', 'still', 'has', 'has', 'has', 'has') with Latinate near-synonyms ('practiced', 'maintains', 'encounters', 'has architected', 'has memorised', 'sustained moment-by-moment'); replaced 'has done' / 'has structured' / 'has the runbook' chains with verb-phrase variation ('has practiced', 'has architected', 'has memorised'); introduced multi-clause complex sentences in the translation field to lift sentence-length variance and FK grade. Engineering content unchanged.
